title: Nihon Shoki: The Age of the Gods
description: Japan's official chronicle: the age of the gods, the sun goddess Amaterasu, and the divine origin of the emperor.
introduction: The Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), completed in 720 AD, is the first official history of Japan. Its opening chapters preserve multiple variant accounts of creation — unique among world mythologies — presenting alternative versions side by side without choosing among them. The text establishes the theological foundation for imperial sovereignty through descent from Amaterasu.

translation.original_text: 古天地未剖、陰陽不分。渾沌如鶏子、溟涬而含牙。
translation.text: Of old, when heaven and earth were not yet separated, and yin and yang not yet divided, chaos was like a hen's egg, dim and dark, containing a germ.

translation.original_text: 天照大神者、光華明彩、照徹六合。
translation.text: Amaterasu, the Great Deity Who Illuminates Heaven, was radiant in her brightness, filling the six quarters of the world with light.
